summary: Dependency-confusion shim with preinstall environment exfiltration via @doaction/shared
details: The package is a thin shim published at version 99.99.99 against a private-looking `@doaction` scope — the canonical dependency-confusion shape designed to override an internal package on installers whose registry resolution falls through to the public npm registry. `package.json` declares `"preinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.js"`, and `scripts/postinstall.js` line 2 simply does `require('@doaction/shared/bin/postinstall.js')`, delegating execution at install time to the `@doaction/shared@^99.99.99` dependency. The main module also re-exports `collectEnv`, `sendToDatadog`, `reportEnvToDatadog`, and `reportHttpEnv` from `@doaction/shared`. Per the package's own description, the dependency collects environment variables and ships them to a Datadog telemetry endpoint. The combined shape — public-registry shadow of a scoped name, sentinel-high version, preinstall hook that fires on `npm install` without consent, and delegation into a sibling that performs environment-variable exfiltration — is dependency-confusion exfiltration of installer environment data.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
